meaning: adj.未见过的,看不见的,未经预习的
examples: For me, a performance is in front of a microphone, over the radio, to an unseen audience.
An unseen person makes the puppets dance.
People sat impatient and frightened in the dark as if an unseen enemy had landed from Mars.
Through these, unseen performers push their lips for up to 20 minutes at a time.
Before Sundance, Sony Pictures Classics made an offer for the film's U.S. distribution rights sight-unseen.
